commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c8c3633e4e3f2b3f16bc65341cf3185948db54c9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Zalo outbound text normalizer and wires it into sendMessageZalouser with unit coverage for horizontal-rule stripping and list blank-line collapse.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows a markdown send containing a fenced code block with a `---` line reaches the new normalizer before the parser's fence handling and loses that line. I did not run tests because this review kept the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs author/maintainer handling for the code-fence and style-offset blockers plus real Zalo proof; automation cannot provide the contributor's real environment evidence.

target.title: feat(zalouser): normalize agent-emitted markdown for Zalo client rendering
target.body:
## Problem

The Zalo personal-account client (mobile + web) renders agent-style markdown poorly in two specific ways that the zalouser plugin currently passes through unchanged:

1. **Triple-dash / triple-asterisk horizontal rules** (`---`, `***`) render as literal dashes / asterisks on their own line - never as a separator. LLM agents emit them as section dividers; recipients see them as garbage.

2. **List items separated by blank lines** render with the blank line preserved, breaking the visual grouping a reader expects from a markdown list. `- foo\n- bar` reads as one list; `- foo\n\n- bar` reads as two unrelated items with extra vertical space. LLM agents commonly emit lists this way because Slack / Discord / the web playground render that style best - Zalo is the outlier.

Both surface to Em Huyen-class deployments (Vietnamese customer-support bots on Zalo) where the message a user receives looks broken even though the model output is well-formed markdown.

## Solution

Add a `normalizeZalouserOutboundText` pre-processor invoked at the top of `sendMessageZalouser`. The transform is conservative: it only matches content that is unambiguously a markdown HR or a blank line inside a list, so plain prose passes through unchanged. It is also idempotent (only removes characters, never adds), so applying it twice is identical to once.

**Behavior:**
- Strips lines that are nothing but 3+ dashes / asterisks (with optional leading / trailing whitespace). Leaves surrounding newlines so adjacent paragraphs keep their blank-line break.
- Collapses 1+ blank lines BETWEEN, BEFORE, or AFTER list items (`-`, `*`, `1.`) down to a single newline so the list reads as one block.
- Hard cap of 2 consecutive newlines anywhere else (defensive).

**Why a pre-processor, not a renderer change:** the existing `parseZalouserTextStyles` path is concerned with Zalo's native text-style payload (bold, italic, etc.), not with the structural cleanup the client needs. Splitting the concern keeps `text-styles` focused and lets the normalizer run regardless of `textMode` (the underlying client rendering issue exists in both `markdown` and plain modes).

## Tests

19 cases in `outbound-format.test.ts` covering:
- Identity / passthrough (empty, plain prose, non-string defensive)
- HR stripping (positive: dashes, asterisks, longer runs, leading / trailing whitespace; negative: mixed-content line, two-dash that is not an HR)
- Inter-item blank-line collapse for bullet, asterisk, and numbered lists, and the mixed case
- Edge cases at list boundaries (blank lines between prose and first item, last item and following prose)
- Hard newline cap
- Idempotency property assertion on a representative agent reply

Full zalouser suite still passes: 176 / 178 (2 skipped, unrelated).

## Real-world verification

This normalizer was first prototyped as a vendor patch in the calling deployment (DOS api-gateway agent template) for the Em Huyen customer-support bot on Zalo. Live evidence in the deployment showed the Zalo client rendering bot replies with broken list structure before the patch, and as tight grouped lists after. Filing upstream so downstream consumers can drop the vendor patch.

## Related

Companion to openclaw/openclaw#84924 (inbound photo support upstream PR). Both came from the same Em Huyen deployment surfacing real rendering gaps in the zalouser plugin.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
